office: House Committee on the Judiciary Democratic Staff
description:
The Democratic staff of the House Committee on the Judiciary is seeking a digital intern for a fall position. This digital internship program includes the production of high-quality graphics, videos, and demonstratives, as well as rapid response work, post production editing, and livestreaming hybrid/virtual hearings and events.

Candidates must possess STRONG technical skills, advanced creativity, and a high level of attention to detail. Experience in Webex, OBS, Adobe Photoshop, Illustrator, Premier Pro, After Effects, LightRoom, InDesign, and Audition a plus. Experience in HTML, CSS, hard-skills technology and big-data analytics a serious plus. Must be a team player and have a strong work ethic. Willingness to take initiative, generate creative ideas, and the ability to work under deadline pressure a must.

The House Committee on the Judiciary's Democratic staff internship program will be providing stipends to fall interns, with additional funds provided to interns with additional demonstrated financial need. The Committee strives to ensure that our internship class reflects the diversity of the country's population; candidates from minority backgrounds are strongly encouraged to apply.

COVID-19 update: Currently, some of our fall internship program will be conducted remotely. However, we ask that all applicants be available to intern in Washington, D.C., as some tasks will be done on site.
responsibilities: Produce high-quality graphics, videos, and demonstratives
qualifications: Possess strong technical skills
Demonstrate advanced creativity
Maintain a high level of attention to detail
Experience in Webex, OBS, Adobe Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere Pro, After Effects, Lightroom, InDesign, and Audition preferred
Experience in HTML, CSS, hard-skills technology, and big-data analytics preferred
Be a team player with a strong work ethic
Willingness to take initiative and generate creative ideas
Ability to work under deadline pressure
how_to_apply: Email portfolio and resume to JudPress.Intern@mail.house.gov with DIGITAL INTERN in the subject line. Digital portfolio, including design and technical work, must be included to be considered for the position.
salary_info: Stipends provided to fall interns, with additional funds provided to interns with additional demonstrated financial need
equal_opportunity: The Committee does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including marital or parental status, sexual orientation, and gender identity), disability, age, or national origin.

description:
The Office of Congressman Jared Huffman (D-CA) seeks a Press/Digital Fellow.

Qualified candidates will have strong writing, editing, and social media skills, as well as basic familiarity with graphic design/video editing software. One to two years of experience in a press or digital media role (including internships) preferred. California ties are a plus!

This position requires the ability to thrive under pressure, multi-task, and work in a team environment. Candidates must have strong written and verbal communication skills, with the desire to come up with creative communications ideas and the ability to work under tight deadlines. The fellow will work closely with the Communications Director and the legislative staff of Congressman Huffman.
responsibilities: Assisting with drafting speeches, press releases, op-eds, advisories, and other written materials
Compiling regular reports on press and social media results
qualifications: Strong writing, editing, and social media skills
Basic familiarity with graphic design/video editing software
One to two years of experience in a press or digital media role (including internships) preferred
California ties are a plus
Ability to thrive under pressure, multi-task, and work in a team environment
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Desire to come up with creative communications ideas
Ability to work under tight deadlines
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should email a resume, writing sample, and two digital samples to huffmanpressfellow@gmail.com with the subject line "(First Name) (Last Name) - Press Fellow."

description: The House Democratic Caucus seeks highly motivated Communications Fellows. This is a paid opportunity. Responsibilities include compiling daily press clips, drafting preparation materials for media hits and assisting as a point of contact for press-related events. The Fellows will report directly to the Deputy Press Secretary. Capitol Hill experience is preferred, but not required.
responsibilities: Draft preparation materials for media hits
Assist as a point of contact for press-related events
Report directly to the Deputy Press Secretary
qualifications: Write, edit, and proofread effortlessly under tight deadlines
Ability to quickly collect, digest, organize, and format diverse information while adhering to strict office guidelines
Unafraid to ask questions and propose solutions
Handle multiple assignments simultaneously with ease and communicate priorities effectively
Generate fresh ideas, propose, and execute clear action plans
Recognize and vocalize key opportunities
Consume a variety of news
Capitol Hill experience preferred, but not required
how_to_apply: To apply, please send PDFs of your resume, cover letter, one writing sample and three professional references to DemCaucus.Jobs@mail.house.gov with your name and "2020 Communications Fellow Application" in the subject line. Absolutely no phone calls or drop-ins please.
equal_opportunity: This office is an equal opportunity employer committed to a diverse and inclusive workforce and does not discriminate on the basis of race, sex, color, age, religion, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity or disability. Women and minority applicants are encouraged to apply.

office: House Committee on the Judiciary (Democratic Staff)
description:
The House Judiciary Committee Democratic staff are looking for interns for the spring semester.

The House Committee on the Judiciary's staff internship program offers undergraduate students, law students, graduate students, and recent graduates an opportunity to experience the operation of a congressional committee firsthand and learn about the important role that our Committee plays in the legislative process.

The Judiciary Committee has jurisdiction over a wide range of legislative and oversight issues, including oversight of the U.S. Department of Justice, constitutional law, federal criminal law, voting rights, immigration and naturalization, intellectual property law, and antitrust law.

Intern assignments will vary depending on the work of the Judiciary Committee each semester.

COVID-19 update: Due to the COVID-19 pandemic the Committee is currently planning on having a remote internship for the spring of 2021. However, public health developments could necessitate the return of an in-person internship in Washington, D.C. Therefore, we ask that all applicants be available to intern in Washington, D.C. if it is safe to do so.
responsibilities: Researching policy issues under the committee's jurisdiction
Attending briefings and hearings on the Hill
Supporting committee clerks and professional staff members
Providing administrative support, including answering calls, delivering correspondence, and setting up the hearing room
qualifications: Undergraduate students, law students, graduate students, or recent graduates
Availability to intern in Washington, D.C. if safe to do so
how_to_apply: The deadline to apply for the spring semester is 2020-10-01. To learn more and apply, click here https://judiciary.house.gov/about/internshipopportunities.htm
equal_opportunity: The Committee strives to ensure that our internship class reflects the diversity of the country's population; candidates from minority backgrounds are strongly encouraged to apply. The Committee does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including marital or parental status, sexual orientation, and gender identity), disability, age, or national origin.

office: Committee on Oversight and Reform (Minority Staff)
description:
The Minority Staff of the Committee on Oversight and Reform, the chief investigative body of the House of Representatives, is seeking a full- or part-time press intern to start immediately. This is a paid position that will primarily be remote, but may require coming into the office occasionally depending on our hearing schedule.

The press intern will have the opportunity to work closely with the Communications Director, assisting with daily press clippings, writing content for social media platforms (Twitter, Facebook, and Instagram), and cutting videos.
responsibilities: Write content for social media platforms (Twitter, Facebook, and Instagram)
qualifications: Creative thinker and strong writer
Familiar with AP style
Detail-oriented
Experience with graphic design and the Adobe Creative Cloud suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere)
Organized with ability to balance multiple projects
how_to_apply: Submit resume and cover letter to oversight.committee@mail.house.gov. Please place "LAW CLERK" in the subject line.

office: Democratic Staff of the House Committee on Veterans' Affairs
description: The Democratic Staff of the House Committee on Veterans' Affairs seeks a full-time press intern for fall 2020.
responsibilities: Performing other tasks depending on the needs of the Committee and the intern's interests
qualifications: Strong oral and written communications skills
Desire to help veterans and learn about the House and legislative process
how_to_apply: Complete the application form on the Committee's website at https://veterans.house.gov/contact/internship and submit a resume and cover letter to veterans.internships@mail.house.gov.
equal_opportunity: In an effort to build a diverse and more inclusive work environment, the Committee strongly encourages veterans, minority, women, people with disabilities, and LGBTQ+ candidates to apply.

description:
The Washington, D.C. office of Representative Sean Patrick Maloney (NY-18) is seeking a press fellow to work remotely this fall and assist with an aggressive and fast-paced communications operation.

This is not a traditional Hill internship. Applicants must have strong writing, editing, and digital communication skills.

Ideal candidates are college students, recent graduates, or graduate students with prior experience working on the Hill, creating content, and writing under tight deadlines. Fellowship is paid and highly competitive. It traditionally runs from September to December. Minorities, women, and LGBTQ persons are strongly encouraged to apply. This job will be remote.
responsibilities: Drafting daily social media content (i.e. Facebook, Twitter, Instagram)
Providing research for op-eds and press releases
qualifications: Strong writing, editing, and digital communication skills
College students, recent graduates, or graduate students
Prior experience working on the Hill, creating content, and writing under tight deadlines
how_to_apply: Please email a resume, cover letter, writing sample, and 2-3 digital samples to NY18PressFellowship@gmail.com with "FALL PRESS FELLOWSHIP" in the subject line.
equal_opportunity: Minorities, women, and LGBTQ persons are strongly encouraged to apply.

position_title: Labor Policy Intern Or Labor Policy Fellow
office: Committee On Education And Labor, Majority
description: The Committee on Education and Labor, Majority is currently seeking a second or third year graduate or law student as a 'Labor Policy Intern' or 'Labor Policy Fellow' for the spring of 2020. Interns and Fellows perform a variety of tasks that support the staff in our legislative work. Applicants should be able to start immediately and commit through May. Graduates and Fellows should be able to demonstrate study, practical or policy experience in issues relevant to the Committee. Fellows must be participating in an established fellowship program.
responsibilities: Conduct legislative research, legal research, and fact checking
Perform technical writing including legislation, memos, and speeches
Assist staff in hearing and markup preparation and execution
Attend hearings, markups, briefings, and meetings to inform staff and Members as they work to craft legislation and policy proposals
qualifications: Second or third year graduate or law student
Ability to start immediately and commit through May
Demonstrate study, practical or policy experience in issues relevant to the Committee
Fellows must be participating in an established fellowship program
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should email their cover letter, resume and internship applicant information form to E&L_Interns&Fellows@mail.house.gov. Please, also, include two short, relevant writing samples in your application materials. Applicants may visit our website at https://edlabor.house.gov/about/internships for more information.
